headline: US Markets React to Trump's Financial Disclosures, Fed Inflation Outlook, and AI Chip Developments
summary: Former President Trump's financial disclosures revealed over $1.4 billion in crypto and stock gains, alongside a $250 million Micron donation. The Fed's Warsh indicated reduced inflation risks, though AI's potential inflationary impact was noted. Semiconductor stocks saw mixed performance, with SK Hynix reporting strong earnings while Micron and Sandisk declined 10%.

bullets.lead: Former President Trump's financial disclosures reveal significant gains from crypto and stock holdings.

segments.text: Trump reportedly made up to $1.4 billion in stock purchases in 2025 and saw a substantial windfall from crypto ventures, with disclosures showing about $1.4 billion from crypto.

sources.title: Trump made up to $1.4bn in stock purchases in 2025
Crypto Brought Trump a Huge Windfall, Even as Many Investors Lost Big
Trump made more than $1bn from crypto in first year back in office
Crypto and stock stakes: key takeaways from Trump’s financial disclosures

segments.text: His accounts are also set to receive a $250 million donation from chip maker Micron.

sources.title: Trump Accounts to Receive $250 Million Donation From Chip Maker Micron

bullets.lead: The US government has opted not to renew a key trade deal from the Trump administration.

segments.text: The US chose not to renew Trump’s trade deal with Mexico and Canada, signaling a shift in trade policy.

sources.title: US opts not to renew Trump’s trade deal with Mexico and Canada

bullets.lead: Trump lifted a ban on Anthropic's Claude Fable 5 AI model.

segments.text: The ban on Anthropic's Claude Fable 5 was lifted, potentially impacting AI model accessibility.

sources.title: Trump Lifts Claude Fable 5 Ban, Anthropic Preps Agent in Microsoft Teams, Elon Musk’s Inner Circle — TITV [Video]

bullets.lead: Nvidia is facing increased scrutiny and expanding its ecosystem.

segments.text: Supermicro smuggling probes are reportedly pushing Nvidia to tighten AI server checks, while the company also funded security firm Verkada.

sources.title: Exclusive: Nvidia Funds Security Firm Verkada
Supermicro smuggling probes reportedly push Nvidia to tighten AI server checks further

segments.text: Nvidia's GPU-network ecosystem continues to lead in data center Ethernet switches, and the company is partnering with Valar nuclear startup for water-conserving data centers.

sources.title: Valar nuclear startup partners with Nvidia on data center aiming to conserve water
Nvidia GPU-network ecosystem tops data center Ethernet switches

bullets.lead: Advanced packaging and AI chip capacity are key areas of development.

segments.text: NVIDIA is reportedly planning to adopt 3D stacking (SoIC) for its Feynman platform, potentially stacking reticle-sized GPU dies on active interposers.
OpenAI has reportedly reserved all Cerebras ASIC capacity, limiting access for other users seeking fast inference, highlighting extreme capacity constraints.

bullets.lead: Inflation outlook remains a central focus for the Federal Reserve.

segments.text: Fed's Warsh indicated that inflation poses less risk than a few weeks ago, despite alternative inflation signs.

sources.title: Fed’s Warsh Says Inflation Poses Less Risk Than a Few Weeks Ago
Warsh faces multiple alternative inflation signs as Fed charts new course

segments.text: Kalshi traders believe inflation peaked in May, with energy prices falling in June.

sources.title: Inflation peaked in May as energy prices fell in June, Kalshi traders think

bullets.lead: Treasury yields rose ahead of the jobs report, while gold steadied amid geopolitical developments.

segments.text: Treasury yields increased in anticipation of the upcoming jobs report, and gold prices stabilized as US-Iran peace talks influenced the inflation outlook.

sources.title: Treasury Yields Rise Ahead of Jobs Report
Gold Steadies as US-Iran Peace Talks Frame Inflation Outlook

bullets.lead: Concerns about AI's impact on inflation and market volatility persist.

segments.text: Cleveland Fed President Hammack suggested that AI could fuel inflation, potentially necessitating rate hikes, while Wall Street worries less Fed talk will spur market volatility.

sources.title: Wall Street Worries Less Fed Talk Will Spur Market Volatility
Cleveland Fed President Hammack says AI could fuel inflation, rate hikes may be necessary

bullets.lead: SK Hynix reported strong earnings, while other memory stocks saw declines.

segments.text: SK Hynix achieved its 11th consecutive earnings surprise, with an analyst target price of 4 million won, contrasting with a broader semiconductor stock decline that saw Micron and Sandisk fall 10%.

sources.title: "SK하이닉스, 11분기 연속 어닝서프라이즈…목표가 400만원"-IBK

bullets.lead: Strategic moves and technological advancements are shaping the memory market.

segments.text: Micron signed 16 strategic customer agreements for DRAM and NAND, representing significant volume and $100 billion in remaining performance obligations, indicating a business model shift.
TrendForce revised its Q3 2026 PC DRAM contract price growth forecast upwards to 15-20% QoQ and server DRAM to 13-18% QoQ, signaling stronger demand.

bullets.lead: Applied Materials introduced new equipment for AI chip manufacturing.

segments.text: Applied Materials launched several new equipment pieces targeting HBM, 3D packaging, and DRAM upgrades to support AI chip manufacturing.

sources.title: 應材推多款AI晶片製造新設備瞄準HBM、3D封裝與DRAM升級商機- 產業

bullets.lead: Major tech companies are making strategic moves in AI and cloud.

segments.text: Meta is reportedly planning a significant push into the AI cloud business, aiming to sell its excess AI computing power.

sources.title: Meta Said to Plan AI Cloud Business Push
盤前股價大漲8％！Meta計畫進軍雲市場出售過剩AI算力- 國際

segments.text: Microsoft is planning thousands of layoffs across its sales, engineering, and Xbox divisions.

sources.title: Microsoft Plans Thousands of Layoffs in Sales, Engineering, Xbox

bullets.lead: The AI industry's rapid growth is drawing strong reactions from leaders.

segments.text: Palantir billionaire Alex Karp described the AI industry as "effing insane" in a heated interview, reflecting the intense competition and pace of development.

sources.title: Palantir Billionaire Alex Karp Calls AI Industry ‘Effing Insane’ In Heated Interview

bullets.lead: Kinpo expects revenue growth in the second half of the year.

segments.text: Kinpo anticipates its second-half revenue to surpass the first half, driven by plant ramp-ups.

sources.title: Kinpo expects 2H revenue to exceed 1H amid plant ramp

watchlist.note: Watch for further comments from Fed officials on inflation and rate policy, especially given Warsh's recent remarks.

x_themes.topic: Agility Robotics' Digit Humanoid Commercialization and Supply Chain
x_themes.summary: Agility Robotics' Digit v4 humanoid is gaining commercial traction with deployments at Amazon and others, benefiting from Schaeffler's crucial role as an actuator supplier and investor. The company is set for a NASDAQ listing via $CCXI, with plans for mass production of V5 and a focus on US-sourced components.

x_themes.topic: Harmonic Drive Systems as a Pure Play on Humanoid Actuator Content
x_themes.summary: Harmonic Drive Systems (HDS) is positioned as a pure play on humanoid actuator content, dominating precision strain-wave gears crucial for robot joints. The company is seeing inflecting humanoid orders and a cyclical recovery, despite competitive risks from Chinese suppliers and potential in-house designs by major players like Tesla.

x_themes.topic: Specialized AI Models Outperform Generalist LLMs in Financial Workflows
x_themes.summary: Research from Thinking Machines and Bridgewater's AIA Labs demonstrates that specialized AI models, fine-tuned on expert financial judgment, significantly outperform generalist LLMs like GPT or Gemini in narrow financial workflow tasks at lower inference costs. The key is capturing tacit pattern recognition through carefully labeled examples and disagreement routing.

x_themes.topic: PENG's Role as NVIDIA AI Factory Partner and Sovereign AI Cluster Deployment
x_themes.summary: PENG is experiencing a strong rally due to its status as an NVIDIA AI Factory Specialized Partner and its successful deployment of Korea's Haein sovereign AI cluster. The company is also pursuing new sovereign AI contracts in the Middle East, Southeast Asia, and Europe.

x_themes.topic: PENG's CXL KV Cache Server and Photonic Memory Appliance Development
x_themes.summary: PENG has launched the industry's first production-ready CXL KV Cache Server for a Tier 1 financial institution and is developing a photonic memory appliance with Marvell, combining photonic interconnects with their CXL KV cache stack for next-gen inference memory architecture.

x_themes.topic: Samsung Files Patent for Novel Dummy Die Structure in High Stack HBM
x_themes.summary: Samsung Electronics has filed a patent for a novel dummy die structure, featuring a three-tier stepped plus curved design and deep groove sawing, aimed at solving reliability problems like delamination, cracking, and warpage in high stack HBM4E and HBM5 packages to improve yield and stability.

x_themes.topic: TrendForce Revises Up Q3 2026 PC and Server DRAM Price Forecasts
x_themes.summary: TrendForce has revised its Q3 2026 PC DRAM contract price growth forecast upwards to 15-20% QoQ (from 8-13%) and its Q3 2026 server DRAM forecast to 13-18% QoQ, indicating stronger-than-expected price increases.

x_themes.topic: Micron's Strategic Customer Agreements & Business Model Change
x_themes.summary: Micron signed 16 strategic customer agreements for DRAM and NAND, representing roughly 20% of DRAM and 1/3 of NAND volume, with $100B in remaining performance obligations. This signifies a business model change towards longer-term contracts and improved visibility, despite ongoing cyclicality concerns.

x_themes.topic: Sandisk Enterprise SSD Earnings Reset and QLC Revenue
x_themes.summary: Sandisk's stock surged 858% due to a 987% EPS estimate increase, driven by strong datacenter revenue from TLC-based enterprise SSDs and expected QLC Stargate solutions. The company's valuation reflects a major NAND earnings reset and its position as a scarce enterprise SSD supplier.

x_themes.topic: Optical Demand Remains Strong Despite NVIDIA NVL72 Copper Interconnects
x_themes.summary: The market's interpretation of NVIDIA's NVL72 as shrinking optical demand is only partially correct; while copper handles scale-up inside the rack, scale-out optical demand remains strong, tracking GPU count one-for-one. This is reinforced by compute utilization being written into contracts, driving demand for optical modules and test equipment.

x_themes.topic: Rethinking NVIDIA's CUDA Moat Amid AI Agent-Driven Migration Cost Compression
x_themes.summary: A report re-evaluates NVIDIA's CUDA moat, noting its three layers (developer ecosystem, libraries, compiler) and how AI coding agents are compressing migration costs to alternatives like CANN or ROCm. This suggests CUDA's premium may be shifting from structural lock-in to execution speed, as tools like Triton and AI-generated kernels reduce its mandatory nature.

x_themes.topic: NVIDIA's Feynman Platform to Adopt 3D Stacking (SoIC)
x_themes.summary: NVIDIA is reportedly planning to adopt 3D stacking (SoIC) for its Feynman platform, potentially stacking reticle-sized GPU dies on active interposers. This move, if successful, could cement NVIDIA's AI computing dominance and trigger an unprecedented scramble for TSMC's SoIC capacity, despite thermal and yield challenges.

x_themes.topic: OpenAI Reserves All Cerebras ASIC Capacity, Limiting Fast Inference Access
x_themes.summary: OpenAI has reportedly reserved all Cerebras ASIC capacity, making it inaccessible for other users seeking fast inference. This highlights the extremely limited capacity and high demand for specialized AI inference hardware.

x_themes.topic: Brookfield and Bloom Energy Expand AI Infrastructure Power Partnership to $25B
x_themes.summary: Brookfield and Bloom Energy expanded their AI infrastructure partnership fivefold to $25 billion, aiming to finance rapid power projects for AI factories. This collaboration focuses on delivering fast, reliable, and community-friendly onsite power solutions for hyperscalers and AI infrastructure developers.
